home_seo_text: PACient Actiu is an application for urology tracking, including a bladder diary, urinary incontinence monitoring and recovery after prostate conditions such as benign prostatic hyperplasia or prostate surgery. It allows tracking urination, monitoring progress and generating clinical reports.
home_seo_text_1: PACient Actiu is an application for urology tracking, including a bladder diary and urinary incontinence monitoring.
home_seo_text_2: It is useful for recovery after prostate conditions such as benign prostatic hyperplasia or prostate surgery. It includes Kegel exercises, intestinal tracking and clinical questionnaires such as IIEF-5 and FSFI.
home_intro: PAC is a <button class="term-link" data-term="prom">PROM</button>-based tool to record health data, track progress, and generate useful reports during clinical, postoperative, or recovery processes.
home_target: It is designed for patients who want to actively participate in their follow-up and share structured information with healthcare professionals.
home_active_text: Continuous tracking helps increase awareness of progress, detect changes, and better understand the recovery process. PAC aims to empower patients to take an active role in their follow-up.
story_intro: PAC supports the patient throughout the entire process: daily tracking, progress visualization, and report generation.
story_prom: PAC uses PROM questionnaires to transform data into useful information for clinical follow-up.
story_register_text: Enter daily data in a simple and structured way.
story_view_text: View charts and trends to understand progress.
story_report_text: Generate useful reports to share with healthcare professionals.
footer_contact_note: You can send suggestions, report issues, or make inquiries.
uro_subtitle: Tracking fluid intake, voiding events, leakage, and urinary control evolution.
uro_register_text: The voiding diary module allows structured recording of data related to fluid intake, urination, leakage, and other urinary events.
uro_register_item_3: Urinary leakage and use of protection.
uro_register_item_5: Distinction between daytime, nighttime, or rest periods.
uro_followup_text_1: Continuous tracking helps identify patterns in frequency, volume, nocturia, leakage, and the relationship between intake and output. This can improve understanding of urinary control evolution.
uro_followup_text_2: The module is designed to help patients actively participate in their follow-up and provide structured data when discussing progress with healthcare professionals.
uro_clinical_text_1: This module is based on the concept of a voiding diary or frequency-volume chart.
uro_clinical_text_2: These records are commonly used to document intake, voiding, volumes, urgency episodes, leakage, and use of protection.
uro_reports_text: Recorded data can be viewed through charts and PDF reports, with summaries by period and aggregated values to facilitate progress review.
module_register_title: What it allows you to record
module_disclaimer: This application does not replace professional medical judgment.
femtes_subtitle: Tracking bowel movements, Bristol type, and intestinal evolution.
femtes_register_text: The bowel diary module allows simple recording of bowel movements, the type according to the Bristol scale, and observations related to bowel rhythm.
femtes_register_item_1: Date and time of each record.
femtes_register_item_2: Stool type according to the Bristol scale.
femtes_register_item_4: Evolution of bowel pattern by period.
femtes_followup_text_1: Continuous tracking helps identify changes in frequency, consistency, and bowel regularity, providing a more objective view of progress.
femtes_followup_text_2: This information can be useful when discussing bowel habits, diet changes, medication, or symptoms with healthcare professionals.
femtes_clinical_text_1: The module is based on the <button class="term-link" data-term="bristol">Bristol Stool Scale</button>, a tool used to classify stools into seven types according to shape and consistency.
femtes_clinical_text_2: This scale helps describe bowel patterns using a common language between patient and healthcare professional.
femtes_reports_text: Recorded data can be reviewed through charts and summary reports, making it easier to assess bowel evolution during the selected period.
kegel_subtitle: Recording and tracking pelvic floor exercise sessions.
kegel_register_text: The Kegel exercises module allows users to record and track pelvic floor exercise sessions, with the aim of improving muscle control.
kegel_register_item_4: Progress in consistency with the exercise program.
kegel_followup_text_1: Consistency is one of the key factors in pelvic floor exercises. Tracking helps maintain the routine, visualize progress, and reinforce treatment adherence.
kegel_followup_text_2: This follow-up can be especially useful during recovery after urological surgery or in cases of urinary incontinence.
kegel_clinical_text_1: <button class="term-link" data-term="kegel">Kegel exercises</button> are a pelvic floor rehabilitation technique used to improve urinary control and muscle function.
kegel_clinical_text_2: Several clinical guidelines recommend pelvic floor exercises as a first-line treatment in certain cases of urinary incontinence, as well as in functional recovery after prostatectomy.
kegel_reports_text: Recorded sessions can be reviewed through activity charts and summary reports, making it easier to monitor consistency and progress over time.
sexe_subtitle: Questionnaires, incidents, and functional progress during recovery.
sexe_register_text: The sexual recovery module allows users to record follow-up questionnaires, incidents, and observations related to functional progress during a clinical or recovery process.
sexe_register_item_2: Records with or without sexual activity.
sexe_register_item_4: Incidents, treatment changes, or relevant observations.
sexe_followup_text_1: Periodic tracking helps observe trends, detect changes, and provide structured information to discuss with healthcare professionals.
sexe_followup_text_2: The goal is not to replace clinical assessment, but to help patients actively participate in monitoring their recovery.
sexe_clinical_text_1: The conceptual design of the module is inspired by questionnaires used in clinical practice, such as the <button class="term-link" data-term="iief5">IIEF-5</button> in men and the <button class="term-link" data-term="fsfi">FSFI</button> in women.
sexe_clinical_text_2: PAC does not reproduce these questionnaires as an official diagnostic tool; instead, it uses its own follow-up structure based on simple questions, scores, and progress over time.
sexe_reports_text: Recorded data can be reviewed through progress charts and PDF reports designed to summarize information for the selected period.
term_prom_text: Patient-Reported Outcome Measures: data reported directly by the patient about progress, symptoms, quality of life, or recovery.
term_iief5_text: A shortened questionnaire used clinically to assess erectile function.
term_fsfi_text: Female Sexual Function Index: a questionnaire used to assess different dimensions of female sexual function.
term_bristol_text: A scale that classifies stools into seven types based on their form and consistency.
term_kegel_text: Exercises for contracting the pelvic floor muscles, designed to strengthen the muscles involved in urinary, intestinal, and sexual function.
privacy_intro: PACient Actiu (PAC) is an application designed to record and track health data in order to facilitate the observation of personal progress in different clinical or recovery processes.
privacy_data_text: The application allows users to record data such as daily logs, symptoms, questionnaires, observations, charts and reports.
privacy_storage_text: All data is stored exclusively on the user's device. PACient Actiu does not send this data to external servers and does not require an internet connection to enter new records.
privacy_backup_text: Users can export or back up their data voluntarily. In this case, the management, storage and sharing of these files is the responsibility of the user.
privacy_use_text: The recorded data is intended to help users visualize their progress and, if desired, share reports with healthcare professionals.
privacy_delete_text: Users can delete their data from within the application or by uninstalling it from their device. They can also delete exported copies or backup files stored outside the application.
privacy_contact_text: For inquiries related to this privacy policy, please use the contact channel indicated on the main project page.
disclaimer_intro: PACient Actiu (PAC) is a support tool for recording, tracking and visualizing data related to health processes, recovery or clinical monitoring.
disclaimer_not_medical_text: The information recorded or displayed by the application does not constitute diagnosis, treatment or medical advice. The application does not replace in any case the assessment, follow-up or judgement of a qualified healthcare professional.
disclaimer_reports_text: The reports, charts and summaries generated by the application are intended for informational and tracking support purposes. Their clinical interpretation always corresponds to a healthcare professional.
disclaimer_decisions_text: Any decision related to health, treatment or clinical follow-up should always be made with the guidance of a healthcare professional.
disclaimer_user_responsibility_text: The user is responsible for the accuracy of the entered data, the preservation of exported copies and the use made of the information generated by the application.
manual.title: Manual
manual.quick.title: Quick start guide
manual.quick.subtitle: First steps to configure the app, start recording data and generate reports.
manual.quick.configText: Tap the settings icon and enter the personal data that will appear in reports.
manual.quick.idNote: You may add a medical identifier, social security number or policy number in the surname field to make identification easier.
manual.quick.configDataText: From settings you can choose the language, create a backup, validate a database before importing it and review general record information.
manual.quick.nightImportant: Night mode does not mean the time of day: it means whether you were awake or sleeping/in bed.
manual.quick.nightExample: For example, urination at 3:00 may be daytime if you were awake, or night-time if you were in bed.
manual.quick.pdfText: In charts and reports you can generate a PDF to share with healthcare professionals.
manual.quick.pdfShare: The PDF can be sent by email, WhatsApp, Drive or printed.
manual.module.uroText: Urination, intake, urgency, leakage and accidents.
manual.uro.title: Uro records
manual.uro.subtitle: Practical guide to record intake, urination, leakage and accidents.
manual.uro.dateText: You can change the day or time to add past records. Tap the date and time box to return to the current moment.
manual.uro.sleepImportant: This is not day/night, but awake/sleeping.
manual.uro.sleepText: If you are standing at 3:00, use the sun. If you are in bed, use the bed icon.
manual.uro.typeText: When selecting a record type, specific options appear: urgency for urination, absorbent type for leakage or accident situation.
manual.uro.validationText: Enter values in ml. For leakage, decimals can be used and 0 is valid when there has been no leakage during that period.
manual.uro.saveText: When the value is correct, the save button is enabled.
manual.uro.editText: To edit or delete a record, tap the three dots on the right and choose the option.
manual.uro.tipMeasureText: Use a graduated container. A 250 ml one is usually enough, although sometimes it may need to be filled twice.
manual.uro.tipScaleText: During the first days, a small 1 kg scale is useful to measure glasses, cups or common containers.
manual.uro.tipLossText: To provide useful information to the doctor, record at least the absorbent change when getting up and all necessary changes during the day.
manual.uro.tipContextText: When leakage or an accident occurs, note whether it happened while coughing, walking, laughing, making effort or due to urgency.
manual.uro.firstDays: At first it may feel a bit tedious, but once common measures are configured, entries become very quick.
